layer: circuit (math) — held-out differential benchmark, no chain
source: P1 adversarial ZK audit swarm (wqknkjp8x), CRITICAL + medium findings on shielded_withdraw_v3

findings_closed.title: Nullifier malleability — pool_key_field was a free private witness, never bound to the public pool_id
findings_closed.before: nullifier = Poseidon(DOMAIN_NULLIF, secret, pool_key_field); pool_key_field is an unconstrained private input, so one note yields unlimited distinct valid nullifiers -> unlimited double-spend in one pool. pool_id was only a dead `_ <==` sink.
findings_closed.after: nullifier = Poseidon(DOMAIN_NULLIF, secret, pool_id); pool_id is the PUBLIC pool PDA (reduced mod r), so the nullifier is a deterministic function of (secret, pool_id) — exactly ONE valid nullifier per note per pool. The on-chain nullifier-uniqueness check then blocks every double-spend.

findings_closed.title: Merkle path_index selector bits never boolean-constrained (MultiMux1 blends, does not select)
findings_closed.before: MultiMux1.s <== path_index[i] with no s*(s-1)===0; a non-boolean selector (e.g. 2) produces a linear blend of the two siblings instead of a left/right choice, letting a prover bend the Merkle path.
findings_closed.after: path_index[i] * (path_index[i] - 1) === 0 for every level; each selector is forced into {0,1}, so every level is a real left/right choice.

benchmark.type: held-out differential (VULN = HEAD circuit minus the fix; FIXED = hardened circuit; they differ by exactly the two security changes)
benchmark.tooling: circom 2.1.9 + snarkjs 0.7.5 + circomlib 2.0.5 + poseidon-lite 0.3.0, sandboxed (docker --network none, build-host != key-host)

unsat_evidence.A1_fixed: ERROR 4 in template ShieldedWithdraw line 162 (nullifier === computed_nullifier)

constraints.delta_explanation: exactly the depth-20 per-level boolean selector constraints; the nullifier binding changes which signal feeds the hash and adds no constraints
constraints.public_input_order: nullifier, merkle_root, recipient, pool_id, relayer, fee, denomination

artifacts.note: This VK is a single-party DEVNET PILOT setup used only to grade the fix. It is NOT the deployed VK.

status: Circuit fix PROVEN at the math layer. Next (pending): regenerate the deployed verifying key from this fixed circuit via the trustless multi-party ceremony (ceremony/run-ceremony-v3.mjs), then redeploy dark_shielded_pool with the new VK + a devnet feature so withdraw is no longer fail-closed. The pool stays fail-closed (mainnet_ready=false) until that lands — no live withdraw path is affected by this change.
